text: Lesson 34
zh: 第34课

text: Adolescence
zh: 青春期

text: What do adolescents respect in parents?
zh: 青少年尊重父母的哪些方面？

text: are often upset when their children praise the homes of their friends and regard it as a slur on their own cooking, or cleaning, or furniture,
zh: 父母常常在孩子夸赞朋友家时感到不悦，觉得这是对自家饭菜、清洁或家具的贬低，

text: and often are foolish enough to let the adolescents see that they are annoyed.
zh: 他们往往愚蠢到让青少年看出自己很恼火。

text: They may even accuse them of disloyalty, or make some spiteful remark about the friends' parents.
zh: 他们甚至可能指责孩子不忠，或对朋友的父母说些恶意的话。

text: Such a loss of dignity and descent into childish behaviour on the part of the adults deeply shocks the adolescents,
zh: 成年人这种失去尊严、沦为幼稚行为的表现，让青少年深感震惊，

text: and makes them resolve that in future they will not talk to their parents about the places or people they visit.
zh: 并使他们下决心，以后再也不向父母提起自己去过的地方或见过的人。

text: Before very long the parents will be complaining that the child is so secretive and never tells them anything,
zh: 用不了多久，父母就会抱怨孩子太守口如瓶，什么都不告诉他们，

text: but they seldom realize that they have brought this on themselves.
zh: 却很少意识到这其实是他们自己造成的。

text: Disillusionment with the parents,
zh: 对父母的幻灭，

text: however good and adequate they may be both as parents and as individuals, is to some degree inevitable.
zh: 无论父母作为家长和作为个人有多好、多称职，在某种程度上都是不可避免的。

text: Most children have such a high ideal of their parents,
zh: 大多数孩子对父母的理想化程度都很高，

text: unless the parents themselves have been unsatisfactory, that it can hardly hope to stand up to a realistic evaluation.
zh: 除非父母本身表现不佳，否则这种理想几乎无法经受现实的检验。

text: Parents would be greatly surprised and deeply touched if they realized how much belief their children usually have in their character and infallibility,
zh: 如果父母意识到孩子通常对他们的品格和完美无缺抱有多大的信念，

text: and how much this faith means to a child.
zh: 以及这种信念对孩子意味着多少，他们会大感惊讶，也会深受感动。

text: If parents were prepared for this adolescent reaction,
zh: 如果父母能对这种青春期反应有所准备，

text: and realized that was a sign that the child was growing up
zh: 并意识到这是孩子正在成长的标志，

text: and developing valuable powers of observation and independent judgment,
zh: 也是孩子正在发展宝贵的观察力和独立判断力的表现，

text: they would not be so hurt, and therefore would not drive the child into opposition by resenting and resisting it.
zh: 他们就不会那么受伤，也就不会因怨恨和抵触而把孩子推向对立面。

text: The adolescent, with his passion for sincerity,
zh: 青少年怀着对真诚的执着追求，

text: always respects a parent who admits that he is wrong, or ignorant,
zh: 总是尊重那些愿意承认自己错了、无知的父母，

text: or even that he has been unfair or unjust.
zh: 甚至愿意承认自己曾经不公平或不公正的父母。

text: What the child cannot forgive is the parents' refusal to admit these charges if the child knows them to be true.
zh: 孩子无法原谅的是，当他们明知这些指责属实时，父母却拒不承认。

text: Victorian parents believed that they kept their dignity by retreating behind an unreasoning authoritarian attitude in fact they did nothing of the kind,
zh: 维多利亚时代的父母以为，退守到一种不讲道理的专制态度背后就能维持尊严，事实上根本没有，

text: but children were then too cowed to let them know how they really felt.
zh: 只是当时的孩子被吓得不敢让父母知道自己的真实感受。

text: Today we tend to go to the other extreme,
zh: 如今我们往往走向另一个极端，

text: but on the whole this is a healthier attitude both for the child and the parent.
zh: 但总体而言，这种态度对孩子和父母来说都更为健康。

text: It is always wiser and safer to face up to reality, however painful it may be at the moment.
zh: 无论当下多么痛苦，正视现实始终是更明智、更稳妥的做法。
